Want to know what your garden style aesthetic is? Watch my upcoming series to find out! Today I’m showcasing english cottage gardens. They are characterized by big colorful blooms, winding and curving lines in the beds and in the paths, and a little bit of a chaotic feel. Designer Gertrude Jekyll pioneered this style and sissinghurt and great dicter are prime examples of a english cottage garden done right! Follow along for the next style!

A beautiful garden brings happiness, peace, and pure relaxation. Even in a small space, you can create your green escape. The secret? Container gardening! Choose a couple of tall architectural plants as your focus star. Then, surround it with a collection of smaller ones with different textures, or even a charming statue. Think of your potted plant arrangements as one big garden sculpture and enjoy! Your mini paradise, right on your patio or balcony. Happy gardening everyone. 🍃 🍃 🍃
